No Poverty.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare access.

Quality Education.reason: As a PhD seminar, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3). Core competency B emphasizes 'sustainability considerations in research,' aligning with Target 4.7 for sustainable development knowledge.
evidence: 博士生為本所之重要資產 (Doctoral students are an important asset)
Students will understand and apply the social responsibility and sustainability considerations in research
協助博士班學生參與學術研究社群 (assist doctoral students in participating in the academic research community)

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to gender equality, discrimination, or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on 'Technology, Innovation & Intellectual Property Management' and 'Innovative research' directly supports achieving higher economic productivity through technological upgrading and innovation (Target 8.2).
evidence: Seminar in Theory of Technology, Innovation & Intellectual Property Management
創新研究與研討能力 (Innovative research and discussion ability)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course title 'Technology, Innovation & Intellectual Property Management' directly aligns with enhancing scientific research, technological capabilities, and encouraging innovation (Targets 9.5, 9.b).
evidence: Seminar in Theory of Technology, Innovation & Intellectual Property Management (I)
習得科技管理與智慧財產管理之專業知識 (Students will acquire advanced professional knowledge in Technology, Innovation and Intellectual Property Management)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to reducing income inequality or promoting social, economic, and political inclusion.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to sustainable urbanization, housing, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency B, 'understand and apply the social responsibility and sustainability considerations in research,' directly promotes sustainable practices and awareness (Targets 12.6, 12.8).

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability considerations' are mentioned, there is no specific evidence linking the course to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or water pollution.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'Intellectual Property Management' and the instruction to 'Respect Copyright' supports the rule of law and strong institutions (Target 16.3, 16.b).
evidence: 維護智慧財產權，務必使用正版書籍

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course aims to 'establish an academic network' and involves 'inviting scholars for lectures and discussions,' fostering knowledge sharing and multi-stakeholder partnerships (Targets 17.6, 17.16).
